title: Chicken Shawarma (Tawa Home Version)

ingredients.name: boneless chicken thighs, cut into strips

subs.name: paneer or firm tofu, sliced — marinate and cook the same way
subs.notes: reduce cook time to 3 minutes per side

subs.name: 2 tbsp thick buttermilk
subs.notes: thinner marinade but same tenderising effect

subs.name: skip — or replace with a pinch of garam masala
subs.notes: cardamom gives the distinctive shawarma note

steps.text: In a bowl, combine curd, ginger-garlic paste, cumin, coriander powder, turmeric, chilli powder, cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper, lemon juice, oil and salt. Mix well into a smooth marinade.
Add the chicken strips to the marinade and toss to coat evenly. Marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 8 hours in the refrigerator for deeper flavour.
Heat a tawa or flat frying pan on high flame until very hot. Place the marinated chicken strips in a single layer — they should sizzle loudly on contact.
Cook on high flame for 3–4 minutes without moving to get a good char on one side, then flip and cook for another 3–4 minutes until the chicken is cooked through with charred, caramelised edges.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes, then slice or shred with two forks. The chicken should be moist inside with dark, slightly crispy edges — this mimics the spit-roasted texture.

To assemble: lay the shredded chicken on a warm flatbread or paratha, top with sliced onion, tomato, a squeeze of lemon and garlic sauce (or curd mixed with garlic). Wrap tightly and serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → paneer or firm tofu, sliced — marinate and cook the same way (reduce cook time to 3 minutes per side); curd → 2 tbsp thick buttermilk (thinner marinade but same tenderising effect); cardamom → skip — or replace with a pinch of garam masala (cardamom gives the distinctive shawarma note)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Cook on a cast-iron tawa on high flame for char without an oven. Use roomali roti, paratha or even chapati as the wrap. Garlic sauce can be made by blending curd with 2 cloves of garlic and a pinch of salt — a quick and accessible alternative to toum.
